data.description:
# RC2b: snapshot-horizon-safe replay (no silent sub-horizon drop)

## Why
A concurrent write on a stale base can receive an HLC timestamp below a peer's snapshot horizon.
A clean git union-merge keeps the event, but replay's positional skip (`idx < start_idx`) drops it
regardless of `source_event_uuids` -> genuine silent data loss. The live store still holds ~11 such
KNOWN-type orphans.

## What
FIRST settle the durability approach (design spike + ADR); the earlier "apply-orphan/dedup-by-uuid"
synthesis was refuted by prior-art review. Candidates:
- Guaranteed-via-rebuild: A1 `.retired` + conservative "fold only settled events" horizon + fsck-detect-then-rebuild snapshot from the full ordered log.
- Structural per-field LWW-by-embedded-timestamp on the ~5 order-sensitive handlers (out-of-order delivery becomes safe).
- Probabilistic conservative-horizon only (cheapest; weakens the guarantee).
Prior art: snapshot = disposable cache / log = truth; GC only past causal stability; decide LWW by
embedded timestamp, not replay position (research captured in session log spirited-balsamic-sealion).

## Scope
Reducer replay + snapshot handling (`src/rebar/reducer/_processors.py` process_snapshot + the positional
skip ~648-702; `src/rebar/reducer/_sort.py`). NOT the reconciler code. Depends on A1.

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] Durability approach decided and recorded (ADR).
- [ ] A merged-in pre-horizon orphan (uuid not in `source_event_uuids`) is no longer silently dropped.
- [ ] Two-clone sub-horizon-append data-loss regression test: RED before, GREEN after.
- [ ] Replay remains a pure function of the on-disk file set (determinism across clones preserved).
data.title: Snapshot-horizon-safe reconciliation (RC2b): no silent sub-horizon drop
